ground_truth.title: Yoga for health: MedlinePlus Medical Encyclopedia
ground_truth.main_content: Yoga is a practice that connects the body, breath, and mind. It uses physical postures, breathing exercises, and meditation to improve overall health. Yoga was developed as a spiritual practice thousands of years ago. Today, most Westerners who do yoga do it for exercise or to reduce stress. Yoga can improve your overall fitness level and improve your posture and flexibility. It may also lower your blood pressure and heart rate, help you relax, improve your self-confidence, reduce stress, improve your coordination, improve your concentration, help you sleep better, and aid with digestion. In addition, practicing yoga may also help with anxiety, back pain, and depression. Yoga is generally safe for most people, but you may need to avoid some yoga poses or modify poses if you are pregnant, have high blood pressure, have glaucoma, have sciatica, or have arthritis. Make sure to tell your yoga instructor if you have any of these conditions or any other health problem or injury. There are many different types or styles of yoga ranging from mild to intense, including Ashtanga or power yoga, Bikram or hot yoga, Hatha yoga, Integral, Iyengar, Kundalini, and Viniyoga. To get started, look for yoga classes at your local gym, health center, or yoga studio. If you are new to yoga, start with a beginner class. Talk with the instructor before the class and tell them about any injuries or health conditions you may have. Most yoga classes last from 45 to 90 minutes and include three basic components: breathing, poses, and meditation. To prevent injury, start slowly and learn the basics, choose an appropriate class for your level, do not push yourself beyond your comfort level, wear clothing that allows you to move freely, and listen to your body.
with: Yoga is a practice that connects the body, breath, and mind.
It uses physical postures, breathing exercises, and meditation to improve overall health.
Most yoga classes last from 45 to 90 minutes.
All styles of yoga include three basic components: breathing, poses, and meditation.
While yoga is generally safe, you can still get hurt if you do a pose incorrectly or push yourself too far.
